tasks.title: Implement filter_emails_by_subject tool
tasks.description: Create SQLite-based subject line filtering that queries cached emails and returns metadata without body content, supporting multiple patterns and optional filters
tasks.testStrategy: Test SQLite query generation with various subject patterns. Test 'any' vs 'all' match modes with multiple patterns. Verify LIKE expressions work case-insensitively. Test sender/recipient filters with partial matches. Test date range filtering with timezone handling. Ensure results respect max_results limit (default 500). Verify matched_patterns array correctly identifies which patterns matched each email.

tasks.title: Implement batch_get_synopsis tool
tasks.description: Build batch synopsis retrieval that processes multiple UIDs in a single call, querying SQLite cache to generate compact text summaries without accessing IMAP
tasks.testStrategy: Test SQLite query with multiple UIDs. Verify synopsis generation from cached body_text (first ~500 chars, truncated to max_chars). Test HTML stripping with html2text. Test batch size limit of 50 UIDs. Verify error handling for UIDs not found in cache. Test synopsis length limits (300 default, 800 max). Verify results maintain input UID order.

tasks.title: Implement streaming and memory-efficient processing
tasks.description: Optimize all tools for large-scale processing by implementing streaming, chunking, and memory-efficient data handling to support 5000+ email folders
tasks.testStrategy: Stress test with 10,000+ email folders using mock data. Monitor memory usage during processing. Verify streaming doesn't buffer entire dataset. Test progress reporting accuracy. Benchmark processing speed.

tasks.title: Create CLI interface and tool integration layer
tasks.description: Build a command-line interface that exposes all three tools with proper argument parsing, validation, and JSON output formatting for AI agent integration
tasks.testStrategy: Test all CLI commands with various argument combinations. Verify JSON output is valid and matches expected schema. Test error handling and exit codes. Verify shell completions work correctly.

tasks.title: Add comprehensive error handling and logging
tasks.description: Implement robust error handling with detailed error types, context propagation, and structured logging for debugging and monitoring
tasks.testStrategy: Test error propagation through call stack. Verify error messages contain sufficient context. Test logging output in both human and JSON formats. Verify sensitive data is not logged. Test error recovery scenarios.

tasks.title: Create integration tests and performance benchmarks
tasks.description: Develop comprehensive integration tests simulating real email workflows and performance benchmarks to ensure tools meet scalability requirements
tasks.testStrategy: Run integration tests against mock IMAP server. Benchmark performance with varying email counts (100, 1000, 5000). Monitor memory usage during tests. Test error recovery and edge cases. Verify output format compliance.

tasks.title: Fix list_email_attachments returning null for emails with attachments
tasks.description: Fix critical P0 bug where list_email_attachments returns null instead of attachment metadata for emails with has_attachments: true, blocking all attachment workflows
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit test BODYSTRUCTURE parsing with various email structures:
   - Simple single attachment
   - Multiple attachments
   - Nested multipart with attachments
   - Inline vs attachment disposition
   - Missing filename handling

2. Integration test with known problematic UIDs:
   - Test UIDs 253062, 253084, 279816 from jobs@mleehealthcare.ai Sent Items
   - Verify list_email_attachments returns proper attachment array
   - Confirm filename, MIME type, and size are correctly populated

3. Test SQLite storage and retrieval:
   - Verify attachments table is created with proper schema
   - Test foreign key relationship with emails table
   - Confirm attachment records are inserted when caching emails
   - Test query performance with index

4. Test cache refresh mechanism:
   - Simulate has_attachments=true with empty attachments table
   - Verify tool triggers IMAP re-fetch
   - Confirm attachments are properly cached after refresh

5. Test migration script:
   - Create test data with orphaned email records
   - Run migration and verify attachment data is populated
   - Test idempotency of migration

6. End-to-end workflow test:
   - Export folder metadata showing has_attachments flags
   - Call list_email_attachments for flagged emails
   - Verify no null returns for emails with attachments
   - Test attachment download workflow using returned metadata

7. Performance test:
   - Benchmark attachment parsing for emails with 10+ attachments
   - Verify no memory leaks in BODYSTRUCTURE parsing
   - Test concurrent access to attachments table

tasks.title: Fix get_email_by_uid missing attachment details
tasks.description: Fix P0 bug where get_email_by_uid response includes has_attachments: true but lacks attachment metadata array, preventing users from knowing what files are attached to emails

tasks.title: Fix filter_emails_by_subject not matching proper nouns and uncommon terms
tasks.description: Fix P1 bug where filter_emails_by_subject returns 0 results for proper nouns and uncommon terms that exist in email subjects, while generic terms work correctly
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit test SQL query generation with problematic patterns:
   - Test pattern "NCHCR" generates correct LIKE clause
   - Test pattern "Yazen" generates case-insensitive query
   - Verify query uses raw subject column, not FTS index
   - Test both 'any' and 'all' match modes with proper nouns

2. Integration test with known failing cases:
   - Create test emails with subjects: "NCHCR Candidate", "Meeting with Yazen", "Kentrail Project"
   - Verify filter_emails_by_subject finds them with patterns: ["NCHCR"], ["Yazen"], ["Kentrail"]
   - Test case variations: ["nchcr"], ["YAZEN"], ["kentrail"]
   - Verify partial matches work: "NCH" should match "NCHCR Candidate"

3. Database verification tests:
   - Query raw SQLite database to confirm subjects are stored correctly
   - Verify no FTS tokenization is removing proper nouns
   - Test COLLATE settings for case-insensitive matching

4. Regression test for working cases:
   - Ensure generic terms still work: "resume", "candidate", "pharmacist"
   - Verify fix doesn't break existing functionality

5. Performance test:
   - Ensure LIKE queries perform acceptably on 1000+ emails
   - Consider adding subject index if needed: CREATE INDEX idx_emails_subject ON emails(LOWER(subject))

6. End-to-end test:
   - Export metadata for Sent Items folder
   - Verify subjects contain "NCHCR", "Yazen", etc.
   - Run filter_emails_by_subject with same terms
   - Confirm results match between export and filter

tasks.title: Fix export_folder_metadata serializing None as string 'None' instead of JSON null
tasks.description: Fix P1 bug where export_folder_metadata returns attachment_names as the literal string 'None' instead of JSON null for emails with has_attachments: true, caused by improper Rust Option serialization
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit test Option serialization with serde:
   - Test Option::None serializes to JSON null, not "None"
   - Test Option::Some(vec![]) serializes to empty array
   - Test Option::Some(vec!["file.pdf"]) serializes correctly
   - Verify skip_serializing_if works as expected

2. Integration test with known problematic UIDs:
   - Export metadata for UIDs 253062, 253053, 253084
   - Verify attachment_names is null (not string "None")
   - Test newer UIDs (279xxx range) still work correctly
   - Ensure has_attachments: true emails have proper attachment data

3. Database migration testing:
   - Test UPDATE query converts 'None' strings to NULL
   - Verify no data loss for valid attachment arrays
   - Test rollback scenario

4. JSON output validation:
   - Parse exported JSON and verify no string "None" values
   - Check that null values are proper JSON nulls
   - Validate against JSON schema

5. Regression testing:
   - Ensure fix doesn't break existing functionality
   - Test with emails that have valid attachment_names
   - Verify other Option fields serialize correctly

tasks.title: Fix search_by_attachment_type to search all email attachments and include email metadata
tasks.description: Fix P2 bug where search_by_attachment_type only searches downloaded attachments instead of all email attachment metadata from IMAP headers, and add missing email UID/folder fields to enable cross-referencing results with emails
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit test attachment metadata extraction from BODYSTRUCTURE:
   - Test parsing various MIME types (application/pdf, application/msword, etc.)
   - Test multipart/mixed with multiple attachments
   - Test inline vs attachment disposition handling
   - Verify size and content-type extraction

2. Integration test search functionality:
   - Create test emails with known attachments (PDFs, Word docs, images)
   - Verify search returns all matching attachments, not just downloaded ones
   - Test that results include email UID, folder, and metadata
   - Compare result count with actual attachment count in test data

3. Performance test with large datasets:
   - Test with 1000+ emails containing attachments
   - Measure query performance with various MIME type filters
   - Verify JSON extraction doesn't cause performance issues

4. Migration testing:
   - Test migration script on existing database
   - Verify attachment_metadata is correctly populated
   - Ensure backward compatibility with existing downloaded_content table

5. End-to-end workflow test:
   - Search for PDFs across all emails
   - Verify each result has uid/folder for cross-referencing
   - Use uid/folder to fetch full email with get_email_by_uid
   - Confirm attachment details match between tools

tasks.title: Increase max_chars_per_synopsis limit to 1500 for structured email data extraction
tasks.description: Enhance batch_get_synopsis tool to support a higher character limit (1500) for synopsis extraction, addressing the P2 issue where structured candidate emails with key-value pairs exceed the current 800 character maximum
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit test the increased character limit:
   - Test synopsis generation with 1500 character limit
   - Verify rejection of requests exceeding 1500 characters
   - Test default behavior remains at 300 characters
   - Ensure backward compatibility with existing 800 char requests

2. Test structured data extraction mode:
   - Create test emails with various structured formats
   - Test extraction of all key fields (Phone, Email, Experience, etc.)
   - Test partial extraction when only some fields are present
   - Test handling of malformed or missing structured data
   - Verify fallback to regular synopsis when no structure detected

3. Integration test with real candidate emails:
   - Test with emails containing structured data at 200-400 char offset
   - Verify complete capture of all structured fields within 1500 chars
   - Test batch processing of mixed structured/unstructured emails
   - Ensure performance remains acceptable with larger synopsis sizes

4. Regression testing:
   - Verify existing functionality with default 300 char limit
   - Test that emails without structured data work as before
   - Ensure HTML stripping still functions correctly
   - Test batch size limits still apply (50 UIDs max)

5. Edge case testing:
   - Test structured data at various positions in email body
   - Test with Unicode characters in structured fields
   - Test with very long individual field values
   - Test emails with duplicate field keys
   - Verify proper JSON serialization of structured data
